Angela Erdmann never met her grandfather. He died in 1946, six years before she was born. But, on Tuesday, April 8, 2014, she talked about an amazing moment when she received a message in a bottle, 101 years after her grandfather had thrown it into the Baltic Sea. People think it is the oldest message in a bottle in the world. A museum in Germany, which is now showing the bottle, gave it to Angela Erdmann.

“It was very surprising,” said Angela Erdmann, who is 62 years old, remembering how she found out about the bottle. “A man came to my door and told me he had mail from my grandfather. Then he told me that someone had found a message in a bottle, and the name on the message was my grandfather’s.”

This man was a family history researcher who found Angela in Berlin after the letter was given to the International Maritime Museum in Hamburg, which is a city in northern Germany.

The brown beer bottle had been in the sea for 101 years. A fisherman named Konrad Fischer found it when he was fishing in the Baltic Sea near the northern city of Kiel.

Holger von Neuhoff, who works at the museum as the curator for the ocean and science section, said this was the oldest message in a bottle he had ever seen. “There are some papers in the museum, which are older than this message, but they were found without a bottle,” he said. “But this one, with both the bottle and the message, is the oldest that we know about right now. It is in very good condition.”

For almost 100 years, police and intelligence agencies around the world have used the polygraph, also called the lie detector, to help catch criminals or find spies and traitors. The polygraph is very popular in movies, where you often see someone nervous and sweating while they are being tested. But soon, this machine may not be used anymore. 

Scientists in Britain and the Netherlands have created a new way to tell if someone is lying. This new method works well in tests, with more than 70% success, and could be used in police stations everywhere in the next ten years. Instead of looking for things like unusual movements of the face, talking too much, or moving your arms—things people often think are signs of lying—this new method watches the movements of the whole body to check for signs of guilt.

The polygraph is very common in the United States for police work, court cases, and security checks for organizations like the FBI and CIA, but it is not used much in Europe. Many scientists and lawyers do not trust the polygraph because they doubt it is accurate. However, the new method made by these researchers seems to work well in experiments.

The idea behind it is simple: people who lie move their bodies more. The system uses a special suit that covers the whole body—like the ones used to make characters in computer-generated movies—to measure these movements. One of the researchers, Ross Anderson, explained that, for many years, studies have shown that, when asking questions, people can only tell truth from lies just a little better than guessing. But the new method has a success rate of over 70%, and in some experiments, it was over 80%. Anderson said the main finding is that guilty people fidget more, and now we can measure this clearly.

The polygraph was invented in 1921 by a police officer named John Larson, based on research by psychologist William Marston. The polygraph checks changes in pulse, blood pressure, sweating, and breathing to find out if someone is lying. In movies, the polygraph looks like it almost never makes a mistake, but in 1998, the US Supreme Court said there was no agreement that the polygraph is accurate. The US National Academy of Scientists agreed with this in 2003.

Do you want your child to be good at sports, join the school team, and maybe compete in big competitions one day? If so, try to have your child born in November or, if not possible, in October. A study found that students born in these months are fitter than their classmates.

Children born in November and October were found to be fitter, stronger, and more powerful than students born in other months, especially compared to those born in April or June. Dr. Gavin Sandercock, from the Center for Sports and Exercise Science at Essex University, led the study. He said that children born in the autumn months had a “clear physical advantage” over others. 

The study included 8,550 boys and girls, aged ten to sixteen, from 26 state schools in Essex. Between 2007 and 2010, researchers tested them in three ways: stamina, handgrip strength, and lower-body power. The results showed that the month a child is born can make a big difference in their fitness, muscle strength, and speed—all important for sports.

The fittest children were those born in November. They had the best stamina and power and were the second strongest. October-born children were almost as fit—they were the strongest and were third in power, with December-born children close behind. Sometimes, the difference in strength and ability between children in the same class but born in different months was very big.

“For example, we found that a boy born in November can run at least 10% faster, jump 12% higher, and is 15% more powerful than a boy the same age born in April. This could be a big advantage,” said Sandercock. These differences could decide who becomes a top athlete because, as the report says, getting chosen for top sports teams often depends on small differences in physical ability.

The study, published in the International Journal of Sports Medicine, found that when all three fitness results were combined, children born in April were the least fit, followed by those born in June. Sandercock said this means that these children could be left out of school sports teams and be less successful at sports.

The small room looks like a classroom. Its metal walls have learning posters on them, such as pictures of the alphabet and a map of Bangladesh. However, it is hard to focus here because you can always hear loud hammering noises, and the air has chemicals that make your throat and eyes hurt.

The children who learn in this small room are lucky. They do not have to work in the factories across the street. For 14 years, a local non-government group called SOHAY, with money from the Global Fund for Children and Comic Relief, has worked in the poor areas of Dhaka to help child workers go to school. SOHAY focuses on children who work in dangerous places, like aluminum and plastic factories, and leather factories. 

This classroom is one of 23 centers SOHAY started in the city. These centers help children catch up on their studies so they can go to primary school. After they start school, they can also come to the center to do their homework and get help from other students.

Alamin, who is ten years old, used to work in a plastic factory. He now comes to one of the centers. His father sells things in the street, and his mother works part-time as a housemaid. The family is happy that Alamin is now in school and not in dangerous work. His friend Rabi says he wants to forget working in the factory. “I like school,” Rabi says.

SOHAY also talks to parents and factory owners to stop child labor, and gives families job training to help them earn more money. “It is very hard to get working children into school,” says the program manager, Mohammed al-Mamun. “Their minds and bodies are different from other children. After they stop working, it can be hard for them to make friends and get used to school.” It is also difficult to help them stay in school, because many of these children stop coming.

Zhorna Akter Sumayya is seven years old. She has two older brothers who both work—one in a restaurant, and one in a local club. After she visited a SOHAY center, Zhorna now goes to a state primary school. Her parents need the money her brothers bring home, but they wanted their daughter to study.

A big disagreement between countries has started about a decision that could change the world’s drug policies. The disagreement is about whether Bolivia’s indigenous people should be allowed to chew coca leaves. Coca leaves are the main ingredient used to make cocaine.

Bolivia has received special permission from the 1961 Single Convention on Narcotic Drugs, which is the main international agreement about drug control. This permission allows Bolivia’s indigenous people to chew coca leaves. Bolivia said that the earlier agreement was against its own new constitution, which was adopted in 2009. The new constitution says that Bolivia must “protect native and ancestral coca as cultural heritage” and says that coca leaves “in their natural state … are not a dangerous drug.”

People from South American indigenous groups have chewed coca leaves for hundreds of years. They believe the leaves give them more energy and can help with medicine. Some people support Bolivia’s decision to defend indigenous rights. Danny Kushlick, Head of External Affairs at the Transform Drug Policy Foundation, said, “The Bolivian move is inspirational and groundbreaking.” He said it shows that any country can change how it follows UN drug agreements if it disagrees with the war on drugs.

However, the United Nations’ International Narcotics Control Board (INCB), which checks if countries are following the drug control agreements, said that Bolivia’s actions are a danger to the world’s system for controlling drugs. Several countries, including the UK, US, Italy, Sweden, the Netherlands, and Russia, do not agree with Bolivia. The UK told the UN that it “acknowledges and respects the cultural importance of the coca leaf in Bolivia,” but is also “concerned that this special permission could cause more coca to be grown and, most importantly, more coca to be used for making cocaine.” Britain said that this permission could “weaken international law on stopping the illegal drug trade and make it harder for countries to work together on this problem.”

On Black Fell, a hill that looks over Northumberland’s beautiful Kielder Water reservoir, a group of people are waiting in a car park next to a modern wooden building that looks like it belongs in the future. This building is Kielder Observatory, an important place for astrotourism in Britain, where people come to learn about stars and space. The people waiting outside are lucky, because many more wanted to come for a night of looking at the stars, but only a few can come each time.

Inside the building, where it is warm from a wood burner and the lights are low, Gary Fildes, the man who started the observatory and is the main astronomer, talks to his team. Fildes used to be a bricklayer and has long hair. He tells his team to be positive and to encourage the visitors. The group talks about seeing the northern lights, but Fildes is not sure they will be visible tonight. Instead, they plan to use their strong telescopes to look at planets like Jupiter and Venus, and at stars such as Capella and Betelgeuse. There is also a chance to see the International Space Station.

Fildes tells his team, “Remember, it’s about talking with people, it’s about making it fun, it’s about making people feel inspired.” Fildes, who is 49 years old, is a leader in the growing astrotourism business in the UK.

A big change for Northumberland happened in 2013, when the whole national park (including Hadrian’s Wall, Kielder Water, and Forest Park—together about 1,500 square kilometers) was named a Dark Sky Park, the only one in England. Dark Sky Parks are special areas where the night sky is protected from light that can make it hard to see stars.

A study in 2013 showed that only 5% of people in the UK can see more than 31 stars on a good night. The International Dark-Sky Association (IDA), which is based in Tucson, Arizona, gives the Dark Sky Park status only to places that work hard to reduce light pollution and prove their skies are very dark. In the Northumberland Dark Sky Park, it is so dark that Venus can make a shadow on the ground.

Duncan Wise, whose job is to help develop tourism in the Northumberland National Park, worked hard to get the Dark Sky Park status. He said, “We usually think landscapes are just what we see to the horizon. But what

When Larry Pizzi first learned about electric bikes about 20 years ago, he asked himself, “Why would anyone want to ruin a bike by adding a motor and batteries?” This is still a question for people who like traditional bicycles. Bicycle shops have been slow to start selling electric bikes, even though they have existed since the late 1990s. Sales in the US have been low. Pizzi, who is now the boss of Currie Technologies, the top seller of electric bikes in the US, thinks this will change soon. Other experts in the business also think this. Well-known brands like Trek, Raleigh, and Specialized now make electric bikes. They believe the market will grow quickly soon. “We’re close to electric bikes becoming normal,” Pizzi said. “There are more companies making electric bikes every month.”

Electric bikes are not as common in the US as in other countries. In 2014, almost 32 million electric bikes were sold, and most were sold in China, where people use them mainly for transportation. Electric bikes are also popular in many European countries. They are easy to find in the Netherlands and Switzerland, German postal workers use them, and BMW sells one for about $3,000.

Electric bikes are different from motorcycles or mopeds that only use a motor. Electric bikes are regular bicycles that you can pedal by yourself, or you can get help from the motor. Riding an electric bike feels like riding a normal bike with a strong wind behind you – the motor helps you go faster or climb hills, but you still have to pedal. Unlike mopeds, electric bikes can usually be used on bike paths and they cannot go faster than 20 miles per hour.

This is what happens to chemists today: they wait a long time for a new element to be discovered, and then suddenly four new elements are found at the same time. Scientists in Japan, Russia, and the United States discovered these four elements. They are the first new elements added to the periodic table since 2011, when elements number 114 and 116 were included. The new elements do not last long and are very radioactive. They complete the seventh row of the periodic table, which now makes many science textbooks incorrect.

The International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) is an organization in the United States that makes rules for chemistry. On December 30, 2015, IUPAC said that the four elements were real, after looking at research going back to 2004.

The scientists who discovered the new elements must now choose proper names for them. For now, the elements are called ununtrium, ununpentium, ununseptium, and ununoctium. These names come from Latin and show their atomic numbers: 113, 115, 117, and 118. The atomic number is the number of protons in the nucleus of an atom.

IUPAC said that a group of Russian and American scientists working at the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research in Dubna and at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California proved they discovered elements 115, 117, and 118. For element 113, which was also searched for by the Russians and Americans, IUPAC said that scientists from the RIKEN Institute in Japan discovered it. This decision means Japan is the first Asian country to name an element. According to IUPAC rules, new elements can be named after a myth, a mineral, a place or country, or a scientist.

Showing a car that can only go up to 25 miles per hour, has two seats, and no pedals or steering wheel might not be exciting at a car show. However, Google in the United States surprised the car and taxi industries by showing the new version of its driverless car. This electric car, which Google is now testing near its office in Mountain View, California, does not have any normal controls, not even foot pedals. Instead, you use a smartphone app to call the car and tell it where to go. There is also a big STOP button between the two seats in case the people inside need to stop the car themselves. The car controls all driving by itself, including turning, speeding up, and slowing down. Google is making around 100 test cars and will test them for about two years. Sergey Brin, one of Google’s founders, said at a conference in California that the car “is still a prototype” but that the goal is “to change life for people who are not helped by today’s transportation.” He said about the car: “You just sit there, with no steering wheel or pedals. For me, it was very relaxing. After about ten seconds, I forgot I was in a car. It was like riding a chairlift alone—a kind of peaceful experience I really liked.” Google says the main goal of the project is to make transportation safer. The car is made with special materials, like foam in the front and a plastic windshield, to protect pedestrians, so Google says it should be safer than other cars for people walking nearby. The cars are being built by a special company in Detroit (the name is not given yet). Google will use these cars to learn more about how to make driverless cars work best. For now, the cars are not for sale and Google will continue testing them.

Galina Zaglumyonova was woken up in her apartment in the center of Chelyabinsk by a huge explosion. The blast broke the balcony windows and smashed some clay pots where she kept her houseplants. When she got out of bed, she saw a big white trail in the morning sky and heard many car alarms on the street below. “I didn’t know what was happening,” said Zaglumyonova. “There was a loud explosion, then several smaller ones. I thought at first that an airplane had crashed.” 

In fact, she saw a ten-ton meteorite falling to Earth, breaking up into fireballs just after sunrise. Officials said that almost 1,200 people were injured, and more than 40 people had to go to the hospital, most because of glass broken by the loud sound made when the meteorite came down. No one died. The meteorite entered the Earth’s atmosphere at a speed of at least 33,000 miles per hour and broke apart at a height of 18 to 32 miles above the ground, according to the Russian Academy of Sciences.

People in Chelyabinsk, a Russian city with one million people south of the Ural Mountains, became very worried. Phone networks stopped working because so many people tried to call at the same time. Videos taken by people in the area showed pieces of the meteorite shining brighter as they got closer to the ground. The white trail in the sky could be seen from places far away, even in the nearby country of Kazakhstan.

Tatyana Bets was working at the front desk of a hospital in the city center when the meteorite fell. “First we felt the wind, and then the room was filled with a very bright light. We could see a strange smoke cloud in the sky,” she said. After a few minutes, the explosions began.

The Ministry of the Interior said that at least three craters, or holes, made by the meteorite were found, and the military was watching them. One crater was more than six meters wide, and another piece of meteorite was said to have crashed through the thick ice of a nearby lake. Local military officials said radiation levels at the crash sites were normal.

An octopus escaped from the National Aquarium in New Zealand. It got out of its tank, went through a 50-meter drainpipe, and disappeared into the sea. This story is like the movie Finding Nemo. The octopus, called Inky, escaped because someone left the tank cover a little bit open. The workers think that Inky climbed to the top of the glass tank, went down the side, and moved across the floor at night when nobody was there. 

Rob Yarrell, the national manager of the aquarium, said, “Octopuses are very good at escaping. I don’t think Inky was unhappy or lonely because octopuses like to be alone. But he was curious and wanted to explore. That is his personality.”

Inky probably crossed the aquarium floor, which is about three or four meters, and then entered a drainpipe that goes straight to the sea. The pipe was 50 meters long and led to Hawke’s Bay, which is on the east coast of New Zealand’s North Island. There is also a chance that Inky used another open pipe at the top of his tank, which then went under the floor to the drain.

“When we saw his tank was empty the next morning, I was very surprised,” said Yarrell. He did not start a search for Inky. “We are sad, but Inky was always surprising us.”

Reiss Jenkinson, who takes care of the animals at the aquarium, was sure nobody stole Inky. “I know how octopuses behave. I have seen them escape in many ways, even on boats. Our security is very good, and there is no reason for someone to take Inky.”

Octopuses have no bones, so they can squeeze into very small spaces, even as small as a coin. They are also very smart and can use tools. In another place, the Island Bay Marine Education Center in Wellington, an octopus would go to another tank at night to steal crabs and then return to its own tank. Another octopus named Ozymandias broke a world record for opening a jar before going to the ocean.

Crispy, full of protein, and usually found under a rock near you—insects have not been eaten as food in most places around the world. However, now more people are starting to eat them. In spring 2013, there will be efforts to make people less afraid of eating insects and to put them not only on special restaurant menus but also in supermarkets. In April, a festival in London called Pestival 2013 will take place. It is supported by the Wellcome Trust and celebrates insects. Eating insects will be an important topic there. The Nordic Food Lab, a famous team from Scandinavia, will run a two-day temporary restaurant at the festival. This team is connected to the Danish restaurant Noma, which served ants at a special event in a London hotel in 2012.

In March, the BBC will show a documentary with food writer Stefan Gates. He will look for and eat foods like deep-fried locusts and barbecued tarantulas. Although some people might make jokes about having insects in their food, there is a serious idea behind this. Many experts think that eating insects can help the environment. Since 2011, the United Nations’ Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) has supported projects to help people in southeast Asia and Africa eat and farm insects. In these regions, about two billion people already eat insects and caterpillar larvae as a regular food.

In 2012, the FAO made a list of 1,909 types of edible insects. With help from the Dutch government, the FAO plans a big international conference about insects as a food source in 2013.

In the next 30 years, there will be nine billion people in the world. Already, one billion people do not have enough food. This growth will put more pressure on farmland, water, forests, fish, and other natural resources, as well as the energy and nutrients we need. The price of meat is rising. It also costs the environment, because large areas of rainforest are destroyed to make space for cows or to grow food for them. Cows also produce methane gas, which is bad for the environment. Raising animals for food makes a large part of the world’s greenhouse gas emissions: 35% of the methane, 65% of the nitrous oxide, and 9% of the carbon dioxide.

The usual eight hours of work in the office have finished. The most important jobs for the day are done. Anything left can wait until tomorrow. At this time, many workers in other countries would think about leaving for home. However, for millions of Japanese workers, the idea of cleaning their desk and going home early enough for dinner makes others think they are not loyal to the company.

For many years, companies in Japan have been able to expect workers to do as much work as possible. But now, the Japanese government is starting to question this culture of overwork. The government is thinking about a new law. This law would make it necessary for companies to give every worker at least five days of paid holiday every year.

Today, Japanese workers can get an average of 18.5 days of paid holiday each year. This is only two days less than the world average. Every worker must get at least ten days, plus 15 public holidays. But in reality, most people only take about nine days off out of their total 18.5 days.

In Britain, workers believe taking two weeks off in the summer is something they deserve. In Japan, many think that even a four-day trip is too much. The new law, which the government will discuss soon, comes after some companies started allowing workers to nap at work to help them perform better. By the end of the decade, the government hopes that, if the law passes, Japanese workers will start to take more holidays, like British workers who take about 20 days, or French workers who take about 25 days each year.

Japan's strict work culture has helped make it a strong economy, and Japanese workers are admired around the world for their dedication. But this hard work often means they do not have time for other things. Japan now has a very low birth rate, and people worry that the population will get smaller quickly. One reason is that couples do not have enough time to start families. More and more workers are getting sick from stress, or even dying from overwork, which is called "karoshi." Even though studies show that working longer hours does not always make people more productive, many workers in Japan still feel pressured to work a lot, like they did in the 1980s.

An old man who lives alone in a hole on the moon is the surprising main character of John Lewis’s Christmas advertisement for 2015. The department store is using this new campaign, which cost millions of pounds, to help raise money for the charity Age UK.

John Lewis’s Christmas advertisement has become very popular and is seen by many as the official start of the Christmas shopping season. This year, the store wants to use its influence to raise hundreds of thousands of pounds for Age UK. They are also asking their staff and customers to work with the local Age UK branch to help take care of older people who might spend the holidays alone.

John Lewis spent £7 million on everything related to the campaign, including a TV advertisement, a smartphone game, and products such as special pyjamas that glow in the dark. In 11 stores, they have decorated areas to look like the surface of the moon.

In the past two years, the store’s ads featured soft toy animals, like a bear and hare, and then a penguin. This year, the story is about a young girl named Lily. She looks through her telescope and sees an old man living in a small house on the moon. Lily tries to contact him by sending a letter and shooting an arrow with a message, but these do not work. Finally, she sends him a present — a telescope attached to balloons — which helps them to connect.

The slogan of the ad is: “Show someone they’re loved this Christmas.” This message is similar to Age UK's own campaign: “No one should have no one at Christmas.” Money made from three products—a mug, a gift tag, and a card—will go to Age UK.

Rachel Swift, who leads marketing at John Lewis, said that supporting the charity is important at this time of the year, and the advertisement makes people think about lonely neighbors.

The campaign uses the song “Half the World Away” by Oasis, sung by a Norwegian singer named Aurora, who is not very well known. The ad cost £1 million to produce. The moon scenes were filmed at Warner Bros Studios, where Harry Potter was also made. The moon set was built by a person who helped create the latest Star Wars film, The Force Awakens.

As with recent years, John Lewis got people excited before the ad was launched by showing short clips on TV and social media with the hashtag #OnTheMoon. There will be a real full moon on Christmas Day in 2015, but this is just a coincidence, according to Rachel Swift.

A nasal spray containing the “love hormone” oxytocin might help children with autism handle social situations better, according to American scientists. Brain scans of children with autism showed that just one dose of oxytocin helped their brains respond better to facial expressions. This change could make it easier and more enjoyable for these children to interact with others.

The researchers said that a treatment with oxytocin, together with behavioral therapies, might help children with autism learn social skills more successfully. “Over time, we would expect to see these children respond better in social situations, be more interested in people, make more eye contact, and be better at conversation,” said Kevin Pelphrey, who directs the Child Neuroscience Lab at Yale University.

Autism is a developmental disorder that affects more than one in 100 people. It shows up differently in different people, but usually means having trouble with social interaction and communication. Right now, there is no effective treatment for these social difficulties.

The researchers at Yale studied oxytocin because it is important for bonding and trust. They wanted to see if it could help people with autism. The results have been mixed. One recent study found that giving oxytocin for several days did not have much benefit. But Pelphrey said oxytocin might help the brain learn from social experiences, especially when used with therapies that help people practice social skills.

“Our study shows that oxytocin changes the brain. This means that, when used with behavioral treatments, it could help people with autism be more social,” Pelphrey said.

In the study, the scientists used a technique called functional MRI to scan the brains of 17 children aged 8 to 16 with autism. The children looked at pictures of cars or people’s eyes showing different emotions. The brain scans took place 45 minutes after the children used either a placebo spray or an oxytocin spray in their noses. The results showed that after taking oxytocin, the children’s brains had more normal activity. Their brains were more active when looking at faces and less active when looking at cars.

The study was published in the latest issue of the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences in the United States.

Benjamin Carle is 96.9% made in France, including his underwear and socks. However, six Ikea forks, a guitar from China, and wall paint without a clear origin stopped him from reaching 100%. But nobody is perfect. In 2013, Carle, who was 26 years old, wanted to see if he could live for ten months using only products made in France. This was for a TV documentary. He got the idea after Arnaud Montebourg, the Minister for Economic Renewal, told people to buy French products to help French factories.

Because of this experiment, Carle had to give up many things: his smartphone, TV, and refrigerator (all from China); his glasses (from Italy); his underwear (from Morocco); his morning coffee (from Guatemala); and his favorite David Bowie music (from Britain). Luckily, his patient girlfriend Anaïs and his cat Loon, who are both French, stayed with him.

Carle said that politicians often give advice and expect people to follow it. He wanted to test if it was really possible to do what the minister suggested and to see if the minister’s words were realistic. Carle explained this to the Guardian newspaper while drinking a coffee that was not from France in a café in Paris, after finishing his documentary.

Carle made three simple rules: eat only food made in France, avoid using anything made in another country, and spend no more than €1,800 a month (a bit more than the minimum wage, to pay for the higher costs in Paris).

At the start of the experiment, Carle was surprised to find that only 4.5% of the things in his apartment were made in France. He realized he needed to get rid of nearly everything, even his lightbulbs (from China) and green beans (from Kenya). When the moving men finished, his home was almost empty.

Carle had no refrigerator (there are none made in France) or nail clippers, so he put food outside on the window ledge to keep it cool and used a penknife to cut his toenails. He replaced all his foreign-made clothes, even underwear, with French alternatives, even though they were much more expensive: underpants (€26), socks (€9), polo shirt (€75), and summer shoes called espadrille sandals (€26), but he could not find any jeans made in France.

During the experiment, Carle searched supermarket shelves for products made completely in France. He learned to cook with seasonal fruits and vegetables grown in France

The city of Ouarzazate in Morocco is used to hosting big film productions. It is close to the Sahara Desert and is the center of Morocco’s film industry, sometimes called “Ouallywood.” Now, this trading city is also the site of a group of four large solar power plants. Together with hydro and wind energy, these plants will help provide almost half of Morocco’s electricity from renewable sources by 2020. There may even be extra energy to sell to Europe. This project is very important for Morocco’s plan to use its deserts to become a world leader in solar energy.

When the project is finished, it will be the biggest concentrated solar power plant in the world. The first part, called Noor 1, will start working in November 2015. The plant uses a special kind of mirror technology, which is not as common and is more expensive than the solar panels many people now put on their roofs. However, it can keep making energy even when the sun goes down.

As the engineers finish work on Noor 1, its 500,000 curved mirrors shine in the desert. There are 800 rows of mirrors, and they follow the sun across the sky, moving quietly every few minutes. When all four plants at Ouarzazate are ready, they will cover a space as large as Morocco’s capital city, Rabat, and produce 580 megawatts of electricity—enough for one million homes.

People have known for a long time that deserts have great potential for solar power. After the Chernobyl nuclear accident in 1986, a German physicist named Gerhard Knies calculated that the world’s deserts get enough sunlight in a few hours to supply enough energy for everyone on Earth for a whole year. The hard part has been collecting that energy and sending it to where people live.

Experts think that by 2020, solar energy will provide one third of Morocco’s renewable energy, with wind and water power also making up one third each. “We are very proud of this project,” said Hakima el-Haite, Morocco’s environment minister. “I think it is the most important solar plant in the world.”

There are now more tigers in Nepal than at any time since the 1970s, according to a new count. This gives hope to people who want to protect tigers, whose numbers have been going down in south Asia for 100 years. The number of wild royal Bengal tigers in Nepal is now 198, which is an increase of 63.6% in five years, the government survey showed. 

“This is very good news,” said Maheshwar Dhakal, an ecologist with Nepal’s Department of National Parks and Wildlife Conservation. The count is based on pictures from over 500 cameras in five protected areas and three wildlife corridors. More than 250 experts and conservationists worked on the survey, which cost about £250,000.

Because people in Asia are getting richer, the price of tiger skins and body parts used in traditional Chinese medicine has gone up. International gangs pay poor people in Nepal a lot of money to kill the tigers. The skin and bones go to other people, who can easily cross the open border to India, where the main dealers are. One big problem is that some high-level officials work together with mafia groups in this illegal trade.

Experts say that the higher number of tigers is thanks to better protection of national parks, stronger anti-poaching actions, and better management of tiger habitats in Nepal, where forests cover 29% of the land. But they also say Nepal must do more to protect the forests and the animals that tigers hunt so that tigers have enough space to live and enough food. 

As the number of tigers has gone up, there have also been more conflicts with villagers. Seven people were killed by tigers near national parks in 2012. In 2011, four people were killed, according to park officials. Villagers are also asking for better protection.

Robert Myslajek stops suddenly. On a muddy mountain path, the scientist finds what he is searching for between two wolf paw prints. “Droppings!” he says excitedly. Seeing wolves is very rare, so even finding their feces is a good sign, even for an experienced tracker. However, it is becoming easier. There are now about 1,500 wolves in Poland. The number has doubled in 15 years. Wolves, along with brown bears, lynxes, and wolverines, are the last large meat-eating animals in Europe. People who work to protect animals from Britain, Germany, and the Netherlands come to Poland to learn how the country has saved this protected animal, which is often shown as evil in stories for children. The scientist, who often appears on Polish TV, says that wolves are amazing animals and can travel up to 30 kilometers in one hunt. “The Beskidy wolf pack is strong, with eight or nine members. This year, we saw five cubs, two young wolves, and two adults. We follow them with cameras in the forest that turn on when they see movement, and by looking at their tracks in mud and snow. In every wolf family, only one pair has babies each year. All family members take care of the young wolves together, working as a team.” Myslajek, whose father was a shepherd, does not understand why people dislike wolves. “Why do people say 'lone-wolf gunman'? Why did we have the Little Red Riding Hood story with the bad wolf?” He is very interested in these wild animals, who stayed in the forests 33,000 years ago, while some wolves chose to live with humans as dogs. Wolves are not like pet dogs. Myslajek says that only scientific reasons—like the need to help forests grow and control the number of wild animals—can help save Europe’s wild meat-eating animals, especially the wolf, which many people do not like. “Natural hunters keep the balance in nature. They stop plant-eating animals from eating too many plants, so trees can grow tall and birds can use them for nests.” Hunting wolves was stopped in the western Carpathian mountains in 1995 and everywhere in Poland in 1998. Now, there are packs of wolves in almost all of the big forests in Poland. The wolves live together with people, not separated by fences like they are in African safari parks.

Illegal downloading is a kind of “moral mess” and stealing, just like taking someone’s wallet from their pocket, says the writer Philip Pullman. In an article for Index on Censorship, Pullman, who leads the Society of Authors, strongly supports copyright laws. He criticizes people on the Internet who think it is acceptable to download music or books without paying. “The technology is so impressive that people do not see the bad thing they are doing,” he writes. “It is shocking that anyone can steal an artist’s work and not be punished. This is the same as stealing someone’s wallet.” 

Pullman’s article was published after music industry leaders met with British Prime Minister David Cameron to talk about online piracy. Pullman, famous for writing the His Dark Materials trilogy, says writers and musicians work hard and are often poor and unknown for many years to make good art. But when they finally succeed, the chance to earn money from their work is taken away. He finishes by saying, “The idea is simple and does not change because of technology: if we want to enjoy someone’s work, we should pay for it.”

Pullman’s words are part of a magazine debate with Cathy Casserly, head of Creative Commons, a group that gives creators more control over copyright by offering open content licenses. Casserly says there are many problems with copyright, which was made for the time before computers and the Internet. She writes, “Copyright usually stops people from sharing, improving, or mixing each other’s work, which was not possible when these laws were created.” She adds that artists should find new ways to share and make money from their work. She quotes science fiction writer Cory Doctorow, who said, “My problem is not piracy, it’s being unknown.”

People from all over Rwanda, and even from some areas in Burundi, travel to the southern town of Butare to visit a small shop called Inzozi Nziza, which means “Sweet Dreams.” They come to try something new—ice cream. Most people here have never tasted ice cream before. It is sweet, cold, and very smooth. This is the first ice cream shop in this central African country. Customers can buy different flavors, such as sweet cream, passion fruit, strawberry, and pineapple. On top of the ice cream, they can add fresh fruit, honey, chocolate chips, or granola. The shop also sells black tea and coffee.

Inzozi Nziza’s sign says, “ice cream, coffee, dreams.” The manager, Louise Ingabire, says that many people are curious about ice cream, and the shop is also helping to change lives. She says, “Ice cream is important. Some people in Rwanda like ice cream, but for many, it’s new. We need to show more people that they will like it.” The shop’s motto is about making dreams come true. Louise Ingabire, who is 27 years old, says, “Before, I did not have a job. I stayed at home. Now, I have hope for the future. I am earning money and I can help my family.”

Butare is a town with 89,600 people and is 135 kilometers south of the capital city, Kigali. The National University of Rwanda is in Butare. Inzozi Nziza has become a popular place for students who want to relax and eat something cold and different. Kalisa Migendo, a 24-year-old agriculture student, says, “This shop brings people together. If you want to go out and talk to a friend, a girl, or a boy, you come to Inzozi Nziza for ice cream.”

Most of the shop’s ingredients come from local farmers. The milk comes from a center in nearby Nyanza. However, the vanilla beans and cocoa are imported from other countries.

They call him the Robin Hood of the banks. He is a man who took out many loans—almost half a million euros—with no plan to pay them back. Instead, Enric Duran gave the money to projects that wanted to create different ways to organize the economy, not just capitalism. 

After hiding for 14 months, Duran is not sorry. Even though he could go to jail for what he did, he says, “I’m proud of what I did,” in an interview on Skype from a secret place. 

From 2006 to 2008, Duran took out 68 loans, both business and personal, from 39 banks in Spain. He sent the money to social activists, paid for talks against capitalism, and bought TV cameras for a media network.

He said, “On one side, social movements were building new alternatives, but they did not have money or ways to communicate. At the same time, our economy kept growing, making a system that created money from nothing.” Duran said he used the loans from the banks to show and protest this system.

He started slowly. “I filled out a few credit applications with my real information. They said no, but I just wanted to see what they would ask me.” After that, the former table tennis coach started making many accounts, payments, and transfers. “I was always learning.” 

By the summer of 2007, he learned how to make the system work for him. He applied for loans using the name of a fake TV production company. “Then, I was able to get a lot.” To be exact, he got €492,000.

DNA taken from the wisdom tooth of a European hunter-gatherer has given scientists a unique chance to learn about modern humans before farming began. This man, who lived in Spain about 7,000 years ago, had a rare combination of blue eyes, black or brown hair, and dark skin, according to the study of his genes. He likely could not digest milk (lactose intolerant) and had more trouble eating starchy foods than later farmers did, who changed people's diets and ways of living when they started farming.

When people began to farm, humans and animals lived closer together, so people probably developed stronger immune systems to protect themselves from animal diseases. However, scientists now think that farming may not have changed the human immune system as much as they thought, because tests on the hunter-gatherer's DNA showed he already had gene changes that made his immune system stronger and able to fight off many diseases. Some of these gene changes are still found in Europeans today.

"Before we started this work, I had some ideas about what we would find," said Carles Lalueza-Fox, who led the study in Barcelona. "Most of those ideas were completely wrong."

The research began after a group of cave explorers found two skeletons in a deep cave in the Cantabrian Mountains in northwest Spain in 2006. The bodies, which belonged to two men in their early 30s, were very well preserved because the cave was cool. Carbon dating showed the remains were about 7,000 years old, from a time before farming reached Europe from the Middle East. This matches with ancient objects found at the site, such as reindeer teeth with holes in them that were used as decorations on the people's clothing.

Agios Efstratios is a very remote island. It is so far away and forgotten by banks, the government, and most of the modern world that there is no way to use mobile data. There are no ATMs or credit card machines on the whole island. Before Greece had its financial problems, people on this quiet island in the northern Aegean Sea lived well. They did their banking at the post office, and every summer, the few rooms for rent were full of visitors. People heard about the island’s beautiful empty beaches, clear sea, and fresh seafood through friends or family.

However, the island still uses only cash. After banks closed across Greece, life on the island became very hard. Now, people have to travel nine hours to a big island to get cash. Also, Greek visitors say they cannot come because they cannot bring enough cash. “Tourist numbers are down 80% this year,” said Mayor Maria Kakali, who grew up in the village with about 200 people. “Even people who were born here and live in Athens, and have houses here, are not coming.”

Mayor Kakali has pushed the government and a big Greek bank, and now they promised to put an ATM on the island soon. But she worries it may be too late for this tourist season. Tourism is much more important for the island than fishing or farming. “We have almost no bookings for August. Usually, people call asking for a room and we can’t help them because we are full,” she said.

This winter may be a little better because 50 workers will stay in the village while they work on expanding the harbor. But there is an even bigger problem coming: the government plans to end a special tax discount that the islands have enjoyed for many years.

Around 50,000 mystery shopping trips happen every month in the UK, according to the Mystery Shopping Providers Association. Because more people are shopping online these days, the need for mystery shoppers is increasing. Mystery shoppers act like normal customers in stores, but their real job is to collect information about how good or bad the service is.

“Shops know that if someone takes the time to visit their store in person, they want service and an experience that is better than online shopping," says Simon Boydell from Marketforce, a company with over 300,000 mystery shoppers. "Our clients want to check how well their stores are giving that experience."

Jill Spencer from ABa, another mystery shopping company, says, "We give each shopper different stores and change them often, so they do not go to the same shop again within three months. Each day, a shopper usually spends up to eight hours visiting five to ten stores. After that, they spend one or two hours writing detailed reports about their visits." For this work, mystery shoppers can earn up to £155 each day. They also get money back for gasoline, hotel stays, and for using their car, which might be up to 20,000 miles a year.

There are also video mystery shoppers, who secretly film their visits with hidden cameras, for example in a button or bag. They can earn even more, about £300 a day. Most of the time, if the mystery shopper buys something in the store, the company pays them back.

Laura, a 50-year-old mystery shopper from Devon, says, “I usually get between £5 and £20 to spend in each shop, so I can test the service at the counter.” Since 2001, she has been paid to visit around 7,000 shops. She says she normally has to buy something related to a service or product that the shop wants checked. “I am given an example situation, like buying from a specific department, but usually I can choose what I want to buy—and I get to keep it.”

Vienna is the best city in the world to live in, while Baghdad is the worst. London, Paris, and New York are not even in the top 35, according to a global study about quality of life. Cities where people speak German are at the top of the 18th Mercer Quality of Life study. Vienna is number one, with Zurich, Munich, Dusseldorf, and Frankfurt also in the top seven. Paris has dropped down the list, moving from 27th to 37th place, just above London at 39th, mostly because of the risk of terrorist attacks. 

The study looked at things like society, economy, health, education, housing, and the environment. Big companies use this study to help decide where to open offices and factories, and how much to pay their employees. 

Helena Hartlauer, 32, who was born in Vienna, said she is not surprised that Vienna is number one. The city government has spent a lot of money for many years to provide good-quality public housing, so Vienna is much more affordable than other big cities. She said, “I live in an apartment that is 100 square meters, in a nice area about 20 minutes’ walk from the city center. But my rent is only €800 (£625) per month.” A similar apartment in London would cost more than £2,000, and even more in New York, which is number 44 on the list.

American cities did not do well in the study, mostly because of problems with safety and crime. The best American city on the list is San Francisco at number 28; Boston is at number 34. In comparison, Canadian cities, with Vancouver as the leader, scored much higher than American cities.

Hartlauer said, “You don’t realize how safe Vienna is until you go to other countries. We also have great public transport. The subway runs all night on weekends, and it only costs €1 for each ride.”

Clay Cockrell is sitting in his office at Columbus Circle, which is across the street from 1 Central Park West. This building is where the Trump International Hotel and Tower is located. In front of the building is Central Park, where Cockrell does his well-known walk and talk therapy sessions. Cockrell used to work on Wall Street, but now he is a therapist. He spends most of his days walking with clients in Central Park or Battery Park, close to Wall Street in downtown Manhattan. He listens to and gives advice to some of the richest people in New York.

“I started doing this because it felt natural,” he said, talking about why he became an expert in therapy for wealthy people. “Many very rich people—the top 1%—think that their problems are not real problems. But they are. Many therapists do not take their problems seriously enough.”

So, what problems do the richest people in America have? “They often feel guilty just for being rich,” he said. “They feel like they must hide that they are wealthy. And they often feel lonely—being part of the richest 1% can be a lonely life.” It seems that F. Scott Fitzgerald was right when he said that very rich people “are different from you and me.”

Therapists say things have gotten worse since the financial crisis and the debates about income inequality, which were made bigger by movements like Occupy Wall Street and the Fight for $15 campaign for better wages. “The Occupy Wall Street movement was important and talked about income inequality, but it made the 1% look bad to everyone,” said Jamie Traeger-Muney, a psychologist who works with wealthy people and started the Wealth Legacy Group. She says the media has partly made rich people feel like they must hide or feel ashamed of their money. “Sometimes I am surprised by what people say. You would never talk about any other group of people the way it is normal to talk about wealthy people.”

Swedish prisons are known around the world for being modern and fair. But are prisons in Sweden too easy? In November 2013, the head of Sweden’s prison and probation service, Nils Oberg, said that four prisons in Sweden would close because there were fewer prisoners than before. This drop in prisoner numbers was unusual. Even though crime did not go down, the number of prisoners fell by 6% between 2011 and 2012, to just over 4,500. There were more drops expected in 2013 and 2014. Oberg said he was surprised by this but hoped it was because of the way Swedish prisons work, with a focus on helping prisoners not to commit more crimes after release.

Kenneth Gustafsson is the governor of Kumla Prison, the most secure prison in Sweden. He says prisons in Sweden are very different now compared to when he started working in 1978. He remembers that at that time, prisons focused more on treating prisoners in a kind and humane way—some people thought maybe too kind. But in 2004, after some famous prisoners escaped, prisons had to become more secure.

Still, Swedish prisons try to treat prisoners with respect, even those who have committed serious crimes. Prison sentences in Sweden are not usually longer than ten years. Sweden was the first country in Europe to use electronic tags for people who broke the law. The country also tries to use punishments that do not involve going to prison, such as community service, whenever possible. These kinds of punishments help lower the chances of reoffending.

The percentage of people who commit another crime after leaving prison in Sweden is between 30 and 40% after three years. In the UK, this number is about double. One reason Sweden has lower rates of reoffending and fewer people in prison (less than 70 for every 100,000 people) might be that you must be at least 15 years old to be responsible for a crime. In the UK, the age for responsibility is lower, and young people are more likely to commit new crimes.

Also, in Sweden, nobody under 21 can be given a life sentence, which is not true in many other countries. Sweden does everything possible to keep young people out of prison.

Many people know they do not get enough sleep, but imagine if we could solve this problem by simply waking up later. At the British Science Festival, Dr. Paul Kelley, who works at the Sleep and Circadian Neuroscience Institute at Oxford University, said that schools should start at different times to match students’ natural body clocks. This would help students think better, get better exam results, and improve their health. Not getting enough sleep is connected to health problems like diabetes, depression, obesity, and a weak immune system.

Dr. Kelley wrote a paper in 2014 with some interesting facts. He said that when children are around 10 years old, their bodies naturally wake up around 6:30am. At 16 years old, they naturally wake up at 8am. At 18 years old, teenagers naturally wake up at 9am. So, a normal school start time is okay for 10-year-olds, but not for 16- to 18-year-olds. For older teenagers, it would be better to start school at 11am or even later.

Dr. Kelley also pointed out that waking up older teenagers at 7am feels like waking up a 50-year-old teacher at 4:30am. He says it is not enough to just tell teenagers to go to bed earlier. Our natural body clock is controlled by a special type of light. Our eyes have special cells that send messages to a part of the brain called the suprachiasmatic nuclei in the hypothalamus. This part of the brain controls our 24-hour sleep cycle, called circadian rhythms. Dr. Kelley says it is like asking someone to control their heartbeat – it is not something we can easily change.
